posts.name: Phil T r o t t i e r
posts.cooked:
I feel like I am in charge of the obituaries: every time I post it is to report a death.

Mr. Edmond Vatan passed away yesterday in Chavignol. He was 92 years old.

I couldn’t find an article about it but may he rest in peace.
posts.display_username: Phil T r o t t i e r

posts.name: M a r k K a p l a n
posts.display_username: M a r k K a p l a n

posts.name: Nathan Smyth
posts.cooked:
His 2003 Pinot Noir might have been the best red wine I ever tasted.

Cellar Tracker indicates that the PN [“Sancerre Rouge”] might not have been imported to the United States since the 2014 vintage.

I hope the vines are still extant, and that they weren’t destroyed.

posts.name: Ian Dorin
posts.cooked:
His 2003 Pinot Noir might have been the best red wine I ever tasted.

Cellar Tracker indicates that the PN [“Sancerre Rouge”] might not have been imported to the United States since the 2014 vintage.

I hope the vines are still extant, and that they weren’t destroyed.

IIRC, he’s no longer making red.

posts.name: Will F
posts.cooked:
I feel like I am in charge of the obituaries: every time I post it is to report a death.

Mr. Edmond Vatan passed away yesterday in Chavignol. He was 92 years old.

I couldn’t find an article about it but may he rest in peace.

RIP to Henri Jouan who recently passed as well. Love his wines across the board.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
His 2003 Pinot Noir might have been the best red wine I ever tasted.

Cellar Tracker indicates that the PN [“Sancerre Rouge”] might not have been imported to the United States since the 2014 vintage.

I hope the vines are still extant, and that they weren’t destroyed.

2003 was the last vintage he produced of red Sancerre. Later entries on CT are folks who are in a muddle.

RIP to the master of Sancerre!

posts.name: Matt Latuchie
posts.cooked:
that’s very sad. been fortunate to drink a lot of his (and his daughter’s) wines over the years and they were always profound.

in speaking with peter weygandt back in 2010, he told me that the greatest white wine he had ever had was Edmond’s 1959 sancerre while visiting in the mid-90s. that anecdote always stuck with me - exciting to learn about the wines longevity.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
that’s very sad. been fortunate to drink a lot of his (and his daughter’s) wines over the years and they were always profound.

in speaking with peter weygandt back in 2010, he told me that the greatest white wine he had ever had was Edmond’s 1959 sancerre while visiting in the mid-90s. that anecdote always stuck with me - exciting to learn about the wines longevity.

You introduced me to this wine, I think it was the first time you came into Orlando. Definitely one of the better wine recommendations I have received from the berserker community.

that’s very sad. been fortunate to drink a lot of his (and his daughter’s) wines over the years and they were always profound.

in speaking with peter weygandt back in 2010, he told me that the greatest white wine he had ever had was Edmond’s 1959 sancerre while visiting in the mid-90s. that anecdote always stuck with me - exciting to learn about the wines longevity.

That 03 rouge though… probably the best Loire pinot I’ve had.

posts.name: Matt Latuchie
posts.cooked:
that’s very sad. been fortunate to drink a lot of his (and his daughter’s) wines over the years and they were always profound.

in speaking with peter weygandt back in 2010, he told me that the greatest white wine he had ever had was Edmond’s 1959 sancerre while visiting in the mid-90s. that anecdote always stuck with me - exciting to learn about the wines longevity.

That 03 rouge though… probably the best Loire pinot I’ve had.

While it’s a generalization, those brighter/warmer vintages can provide nice tailwinds for Loire reds.
